title: SigmaSeparationEquivalences

tex_statement.html:
1. By Proposition claim_3_23 we have that $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ is equivalent to either of the following:
    1. every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$;
    2. every path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$.
  2. Proposition claim_3_23 also shows that if $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$ holds then:
    1. there exists a (shortest) $C$-$\sigma$-open path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$;
    2. there exists a (shortest) $C$-$\sigma$-open walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ such that all its colliders lie in $C$.

In practice we usually check if every path is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked or not. This is because there are, in contrast to walks, only a finite number of paths in a (finite) graph. In proofs, though, it often is easier to make use of walks, since these can be concatenated into walks (while one cannot in general concatenate two paths and again obtain a path).

tex_proof.html:
Remark — SigmaSeparationEquivalences
  1. By Proposition claim_3_23 we have that $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ is equivalent to either of the following:
    1. every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$;
    2. every path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$.
  2. Proposition claim_3_23 also shows that if $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$ holds then:
    1. there exists a (shortest) $C$-$\sigma$-open path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$;
    2. there exists a (shortest) $C$-$\sigma$-open walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ such that all its colliders lie in $C$.

In practice we usually check if every path is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked or not. This is because there are, in contrast to walks, only a finite number of paths in a (finite) graph. In proofs, though, it often is easier to make use of walks, since these can be concatenated into walks (while one cannot in general concatenate two paths and again obtain a path).

Proof.

Both items are direct corollaries of Proposition claim_3_23 (the LN's $\mathtt{prp\!:\!sigma\_opens}$, recalling the three-way equivalence: for $C \subseteq J \cup V$ and $w_1, w_2 \in J \cup V$, the existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open path between $w_1$ and $w_2$, the existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk between $w_1$ and $w_2$, and the existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk between $w_1$ and $w_2$ all of whose colliders lie in $C$ are mutually equivalent). We treat the two items in turn.

A remark on “(shortest)”.\; The LN's parenthetical “(shortest)” in clauses 2(a) and 2(b) is a strengthening of plain existence that is not part of the substantive content of the present equivalences. Given any $C$-$\sigma$-open path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ (resp.\ any $C$-$\sigma$-open walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ with all colliders in $C$), the set \[ \big\{ \ell \in \mathbb{N} : \text{there is such a witness of length } \ell \big\} \] is a non-empty subset of $\mathbb{N}$ and therefore (by the well-ordering of $\mathbb{N}$, equivalently $\mathtt{Nat.find}$ on the length-of-witness predicate) admits a least element. The corresponding witness is a shortest one. We therefore prove plain existence below; the strengthening to “shortest” follows by this one-line well-foundedness argument and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually need length-minimality.

Item 1.\; We prove that the following three statements are mutually equivalent:

  1. (0) $A \isPerp_G B \given C$;
  2. (1) every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$;
  3. (2) every path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$.

It suffices to establish $(0) \iff (1)$ and $(1) \iff (2)$; transitivity of $\iff$ then closes all three pairs.

$(0) \iff (1)$.\; This is the defining clause of $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ (see def_3_18, clause (1)): by definition, $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ means “every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$”. The biconditional is therefore an unfolding of the defining equation, not a content-bearing step; we record it as a separate sub-equivalence only because the LN's clause 1(a) names this universal explicitly alongside the predicate.

$(1) \implies (2)$.\; A path is in particular a walk, so the universal over walks restricts to the universal over paths. Concretely, suppose $(1)$ holds and let $\pi$ be any path from a node $v \in A$ to a node $w \in J \cup B$. Then $\pi$ is in particular a walk from $v \in A$ to $w \in J \cup B$, hence by $(1)$ it is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$. Since the path $\pi$ was arbitrary, $(2)$ holds.

$(2) \implies (1)$.\; By contrapositive. Suppose $(1)$ fails, i.e.\ there exist $v \in A$, $w \in J \cup B$, and a walk $\pi$ from $v$ to $w$ that is not $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$ – equivalently, $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open. Apply claim_3_23 (the implication from clause (2) to clause (1) of $\mathtt{prp\!:\!sigma\_opens}$, instantiated at $w_1 := v$ and $w_2 := w$, noting $v \in A \subseteq J \cup V$ and $w \in J \cup B \subseteq J \cup V$): from the existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk between $v$ and $w$ we obtain the existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open path between $v$ and $w$. Pick such a path $\pi'$; then $\pi'$ is a path from $v \in A$ to $w \in J \cup B$ that is not $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$, contradicting $(2)$. Hence $(2) \implies (1)$, and this is the only step of item 1 that uses claim_3_23.

This closes item 1.

Item 2.\; We prove that the following three statements are mutually equivalent:

  1. (0) $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$;
  2. (1) there exists a $C$-$\sigma$-open path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$;
  3. (2) there exists a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ such that all its colliders lie in $C$.

Note the deliberate asymmetry between $(1)$ and $(2)$: clause $(1)$ asks only for a $C$-$\sigma$-open path (no collider-control conjunct), while clause $(2)$ adds the conjunct “all colliders of the witness walk lie in $C$”. This mirrors clauses (1) and (3) of $\mathtt{prp\!:\!sigma\_opens}$ exactly and is what claim_3_23 delivers between them; we preserve it. Again it suffices to establish $(0) \iff (1)$ and $(1) \iff (2)$.

$(0) \implies (1)$.\; By def_3_18, clause (2), $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$ is the LN's shorthand for the negation $\neg \lp A \isPerp_G B \given C \rp$. Unfolding $\isPerp_G$ via clause (1) of the same definition, this negation reads: not every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. Pushing the negation classically through the three universal quantifiers (one each for $v$, $w$, and the walk $\pi$), there exist $v \in A$, $w \in J \cup B$, and a walk $\pi$ from $v$ to $w$ such that $\pi$ is not $C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$ – equivalently, $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open. Now apply claim_3_23 (the implication from clause (2) to clause (1) of $\mathtt{prp\!:\!sigma\_opens}$, with $w_1 := v$ and $w_2 := w$): from the $C$-$\sigma$-open walk $\pi$ between $v$ and $w$ we obtain a $C$-$\sigma$-open path $\pi'$ between $v$ and $w$. The triple $(v, w, \pi')$ witnesses $(1)$: $v \in A$, $w \in J \cup B$, and $\pi'$ is a $C$-$\sigma$-open path from $v$ to $w$.

$(1) \implies (0)$.\; Suppose $(1)$: pick $v \in A$, $w \in J \cup B$, and a $C$-$\sigma$-open path $\pi$ from $v$ to $w$. A path is in particular a walk, so $\pi$ is a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk from $v \in A$ to $w \in J \cup B$. The triple $(v, w, \pi)$ thereby witnesses the failure of the universal “every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked” – there is a walk between such nodes that is not $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. Hence $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ fails, i.e.\ $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$ holds (def_3_18, clause (2)), which is $(0)$. This direction does not require claim_3_23; it is the trivial path-to-walk weakening composed with the definition of $\nisPerp_G$.

$(1) \iff (2)$.\; This is a direct application of claim_3_23, packaged inside the surrounding existentials $\exists v \in A, \exists w \in J \cup B$.

Concretely, fix any $v \in A \subseteq J \cup V$ and $w \in J \cup B \subseteq J \cup V$. By claim_3_23 (the equivalence between clauses (1) and (3) of $\mathtt{prp\!:\!sigma\_opens}$, instantiated at $w_1 := v$ and $w_2 := w$), \[ \big( \exists \text{ $C$-$\sigma$-open path between $v$ and $w$ in $G$} \big) \;\iff\; \big( \exists \text{ $C$-$\sigma$-open walk between $v$ and $w$ in $G$ with all colliders in $C$} \big). \] This is a pointwise (in $v$ and $w$) equivalence. Quantifying both sides existentially over $v \in A$ and $w \in J \cup B$, the existential quantifiers distribute through the equivalence: \[ \big( \exists v \in A,\, \exists w \in J \cup B,\, \exists \text{ $C$-$\sigma$-open path from $v$ to $w$} \big) \iff \big( \exists v \in A,\, \exists w \in J \cup B,\, \exists \text{ $C$-$\sigma$-open walk from $v$ to $w$ with colliders in $C$} \big). \] The left-hand side is $(1)$ and the right-hand side is $(2)$. (The asymmetry flagged above is intact: the path side of $\mathtt{prp\!:\!sigma\_opens}$ carries no collider-control conjunct – clause (1) – and the walk side carries the full collider-control conjunct – clause (3) – and we transport both unchanged.)

This closes item 2, and the proof is complete.

lean_code_with_comments:
/-- claim_3_24 (item 1, `SigmaSeparationEquivalences`): the
three-way equivalence between the `IsISigmaSeparated` predicate,
the unfolded "every walk from a node in `A` to a node in
`G.J ∪ B` is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked" universal (definitionally
identical to the predicate), and the LN's substantive "every
*path* from a node in `A` to a node in `G.J ∪ B` is
$C$-$\sigma$-blocked" universal. The 0 $\leftrightarrow$ 1
direction is `Iff.rfl` against `isISigmaSeparated_iff`; the
substantive 1 $\leftrightarrow$ 2 direction follows from
`sigmaOpens_TFAE`. Pairwise extraction is
`(G.isISigmaSeparated_TFAE A B C).out i j` for `i, j ∈ {0, 1, 2}`. -/
theorem isISigmaSeparated_TFAE (G : CDMG α) (A B C : Set α) :
    List.TFAE
      [ G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C,
        ∀ ⦃v w : α⦄, v ∈ A → w ∈ G.J ∪ B → ∀ (π : Walk G v w),
          π.IsSigmaBlocked C,
        ∀ ⦃v w : α⦄, v ∈ A → w ∈ G.J ∪ B → ∀ (π : Walk G v w),
          π.IsPath → π.IsSigmaBlocked C ] := by
  classical
  -- (0) ↔ (1): the predicate `IsISigmaSeparated` is definitionally
  -- the unfolded "every walk" universal -- `isISigmaSeparated_iff`
  -- is `Iff.rfl`. We record both directions explicitly so that
  -- `tfae_finish` can compose them.
  tfae_have h₁₂ : 1 → 2 := fun h => h
  tfae_have h₂₁ : 2 → 1 := fun h => h
  -- (1) ⇒ (2): a path is in particular a walk, so the universal
  -- over walks restricts to the universal over paths.
  tfae_have _h₂₃ : 2 → 3 := fun h v w hv hw π _hPath => h hv hw π
  -- (2) ⇒ (1): contrapositive via `sigmaOpens_TFAE` (the LN's
  -- prp:sigma_opens, walk-existence → path-existence -- here used
  -- in the "if any walk is σ-open then some path is σ-open" form).
  tfae_have _h₃₂ : 3 → 2 := by
    intro h v w hv hw π
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen]
    intro hOpen
    have hWalk : ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsSigmaOpen C := ⟨π, hOpen⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hPath', hOpen'⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 1 0).mp hWalk
    have hBlocked := h hv hw π' hPath'
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen] at hBlocked
    exact hBlocked hOpen'
  tfae_finish

/-- claim_3_24 (item 2, `SigmaSeparationEquivalences`): under
the LN's $\nisPerp_G$ (`IsNotISigmaSeparated`), the three-way
equivalence between the negation predicate, the existence of a
$C$-$\sigma$-open *path* from a node in `A` to a node in
`G.J ∪ B`, and the existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open *walk* from
a node in `A` to a node in `G.J ∪ B` all of whose colliders lie
in `C`. Substantive direction follows from `sigmaOpens_TFAE`
via De Morgan on `isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen`. The LN's
parenthetical "(shortest)" is dropped; downstream consumers
derive minimality via `Nat.find` on `Walk.length`. Pairwise
extraction is `(G.isNotISigmaSeparated_TFAE A B C).out i j` for
`i, j ∈ {0, 1, 2}`. -/
theorem isNotISigmaSeparated_TFAE (G : CDMG α) (A B C : Set α) :
    List.TFAE
      [ G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C,
        ∃ (v w : α) (π : Walk G v w),
          v ∈ A ∧ w ∈ G.J ∪ B ∧ π.IsPath ∧ π.IsSigmaOpen C,
        ∃ (v w : α) (π : Walk G v w),
          v ∈ A ∧ w ∈ G.J ∪ B ∧ π.IsSigmaOpen C ∧
            ∀ k, π.IsColliderAt k → π.nodeAt k ∈ C ] := by
  classical
  -- (0) ⇒ (1): the substantive direction. Push the negation
  -- inside `IsNotISigmaSeparated` through `IsISigmaSeparated`'s
  -- three nested universals, flip "not σ-blocked" to "σ-open"
  -- via De Morgan, then promote σ-open walk to σ-open path via
  -- `sigmaOpens_TFAE.out 1 0`.
  tfae_have _h₁₂ : 1 → 2 := by
    intro hNot
    simp only [isNotISigmaSeparated_iff, isISigmaSeparated_iff] at hNot
    push Not at hNot
    obtain ⟨v, w, hv, hw, π, hNotBlocked⟩ := hNot
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen, not_not] at hNotBlocked
    have hWalk : ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsSigmaOpen C := ⟨π, hNotBlocked⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hPath', hOpen'⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 1 0).mp hWalk
    exact ⟨v, w, π', hv, hw, hPath', hOpen'⟩
  -- (1) ⇒ (0): the trivial converse. A σ-open path is in
  -- particular a σ-open walk between `v ∈ A` and `w ∈ G.J ∪ B`,
  -- witnessing the failure of `IsISigmaSeparated`.
  tfae_have _h₂₁ : 2 → 1 := by
    rintro ⟨v, w, π, hv, hw, _hPath, hOpen⟩
    rw [isNotISigmaSeparated_iff]
    intro hSep
    have hBlocked := hSep hv hw π
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen] at hBlocked
    exact hBlocked hOpen
  -- (1) ⇔ (2): pointwise application of `sigmaOpens_TFAE.out 0 2`
  -- distributed through the surrounding `∃ v ∈ A, ∃ w ∈ G.J ∪ B`
  -- existentials.
  tfae_have _h₂₃ : 2 → 3 := by
    rintro ⟨v, w, π, hv, hw, hPath, hOpen⟩
    have hPathPair :
        ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsPath ∧ π.IsSigmaOpen C := ⟨π, hPath, hOpen⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hOpen', hColl⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 0 2).mp hPathPair
    exact ⟨v, w, π', hv, hw, hOpen', hColl⟩
  tfae_have _h₃₂ : 3 → 2 := by
    rintro ⟨v, w, π, hv, hw, hOpen, hColl⟩
    have hWalkColl :
        ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsSigmaOpen C ∧
          ∀ k, π.IsColliderAt k → π.nodeAt k ∈ C := ⟨π, hOpen, hColl⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hPath', hOpen'⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 2 0).mp hWalkColl
    exact ⟨v, w, π', hv, hw, hPath', hOpen'⟩
  tfae_finish
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem isISigmaSeparated_TFAE (G : CDMG α) (A B C : Set α) :
    List.TFAE
      [ G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C,
        ∀ ⦃v w : α⦄, v ∈ A → w ∈ G.J ∪ B → ∀ (π : Walk G v w),
          π.IsSigmaBlocked C,
        ∀ ⦃v w : α⦄, v ∈ A → w ∈ G.J ∪ B → ∀ (π : Walk G v w),
          π.IsPath → π.IsSigmaBlocked C ] := by
  classical

  tfae_have h₁₂ : 1 → 2 := fun h => h
  tfae_have h₂₁ : 2 → 1 := fun h => h

  tfae_have _h₂₃ : 2 → 3 := fun h v w hv hw π _hPath => h hv hw π

  tfae_have _h₃₂ : 3 → 2 := by
    intro h v w hv hw π
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen]
    intro hOpen
    have hWalk : ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsSigmaOpen C := ⟨π, hOpen⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hPath', hOpen'⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 1 0).mp hWalk
    have hBlocked := h hv hw π' hPath'
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen] at hBlocked
    exact hBlocked hOpen'
  tfae_finish

theorem isNotISigmaSeparated_TFAE (G : CDMG α) (A B C : Set α) :
    List.TFAE
      [ G.IsNotISigmaSeparated A B C,
        ∃ (v w : α) (π : Walk G v w),
          v ∈ A ∧ w ∈ G.J ∪ B ∧ π.IsPath ∧ π.IsSigmaOpen C,
        ∃ (v w : α) (π : Walk G v w),
          v ∈ A ∧ w ∈ G.J ∪ B ∧ π.IsSigmaOpen C ∧
            ∀ k, π.IsColliderAt k → π.nodeAt k ∈ C ] := by
  classical

  tfae_have _h₁₂ : 1 → 2 := by
    intro hNot
    simp only [isNotISigmaSeparated_iff, isISigmaSeparated_iff] at hNot
    push Not at hNot
    obtain ⟨v, w, hv, hw, π, hNotBlocked⟩ := hNot
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen, not_not] at hNotBlocked
    have hWalk : ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsSigmaOpen C := ⟨π, hNotBlocked⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hPath', hOpen'⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 1 0).mp hWalk
    exact ⟨v, w, π', hv, hw, hPath', hOpen'⟩

  tfae_have _h₂₁ : 2 → 1 := by
    rintro ⟨v, w, π, hv, hw, _hPath, hOpen⟩
    rw [isNotISigmaSeparated_iff]
    intro hSep
    have hBlocked := hSep hv hw π
    rw [Walk.isSigmaBlocked_iff_not_isSigmaOpen] at hBlocked
    exact hBlocked hOpen

  tfae_have _h₂₃ : 2 → 3 := by
    rintro ⟨v, w, π, hv, hw, hPath, hOpen⟩
    have hPathPair :
        ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsPath ∧ π.IsSigmaOpen C := ⟨π, hPath, hOpen⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hOpen', hColl⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 0 2).mp hPathPair
    exact ⟨v, w, π', hv, hw, hOpen', hColl⟩
  tfae_have _h₃₂ : 3 → 2 := by
    rintro ⟨v, w, π, hv, hw, hOpen, hColl⟩
    have hWalkColl :
        ∃ π : Walk G v w, π.IsSigmaOpen C ∧
          ∀ k, π.IsColliderAt k → π.nodeAt k ∈ C := ⟨π, hOpen, hColl⟩
    obtain ⟨π', hPath', hOpen'⟩ :=
      ((G.sigmaOpens_TFAE C v w).out 2 0).mp hWalkColl
    exact ⟨v, w, π', hv, hw, hPath', hOpen'⟩
  tfae_finish
lean_explanation:
The file states two TFAEs ("the following are equivalent") inside `namespace Causality.CDMG`, one per numbered item of the LN's Remark. Both are stated for an ambient vertex type `α : Type*` (the silent carrier the LN assumes throughout Chapter 3), a graph `G : CDMG α` (the LN's conditional directed mixed graph), and three vertex sets `A B C : Set α`. The set `G.J` is the graph's collection of input nodes; the LN's set $J \cup B$ becomes `G.J ∪ B`.

Walks are values of `Walk G v w`. The predicate `π.IsSigmaBlocked C` is the LN's "$C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$"; its De Morgan dual `π.IsSigmaOpen C` is "$C$-$\sigma$-open". `π.IsPath` asserts no repeated vertices, the LN's distinction between walks and paths. The two functions `π.IsColliderAt k` and `π.nodeAt k` together let the third TFAE clause say "every collider position $k$ along $\pi$ has its node in $C$".

`isISigmaSeparated_TFAE` (item 1) lists three clauses: the named predicate `G.IsISigmaSeparated A B C`; the unfolded "every walk" universal (literally the same expression as the predicate's body, so positions 0 ↔ 1 reduces by `Iff.rfl`); and the LN's substantive "every path" universal. The two universals use Mathlib's strict-implicit binders `⦃v w : α⦄` — these display the vertices as ordinary explicit arguments at use sites but elide them when the term is fully applied, mirroring the predicate's signature exactly so the `Iff.rfl` collapse goes through.

`isNotISigmaSeparated_TFAE` (item 2) is the parallel TFAE under the LN's $\nisPerp_G$. Its three clauses are the negation predicate, the existence of a $\sigma$-open path between vertices in $A$ and $J \cup B$, and the existence of a $\sigma$-open walk with all colliders in $C$. The existentials use ordinary explicit binders `(v w : α) (π : Walk G v w)` so call sites can `obtain ⟨v, w, π, …⟩` without fishing the vertices out of `π`'s type. Both theorems are pure restatements: pair-wise equivalences are extracted at call sites by `(G.isISigmaSeparated_TFAE A B C).out i j`.
design_choices:
**Why `List.TFAE` rather than a single biconditional.** The LN's "is equivalent to either of the following" lists the predicate and two sub-clauses on equal footing. A biconditional would have to discard one sub-clause or fold both into an awkward conjunction; TFAE over three entries preserves the LN's numbered-list shape and gives a uniform `.out i j` call-site idiom, matching the established convention of the preceding claim_3_23 (`sigmaOpens_TFAE`).

**Why two separate TFAEs, not one bundled five-clause TFAE.** Item 1 is about `IsISigmaSeparated`; item 2 is about its negation. A bundled list would conflate the two — a proposition and its negation cannot be pairwise equivalent, so `List.TFAE` semantics would fail. Conversely, dissolving each item into separate biconditionals would proliferate the API and lose the LN's local enumeration. Two TFAEs, one per numbered item, is the unique shape that respects both constraints.

**Predicate name in position 0, unfolded universal still listed.** The LN's clause 1(a) names the "every walk is $\sigma$-blocked" universal explicitly even though it is definitionally the predicate; we list it as a separate TFAE entry so the LN's numbered structure is preserved one-to-one. The redundancy is a feature: callers wanting the unfolded surface write `.out 0 1` for a one-line `Iff.rfl`, while the substantive content lives at `.out 0 2` (or `.out 1 2`).

**`G.J ∪ B`, not `B ∪ G.J`.** The union order matches the LN's "from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$" and matches the body of `IsISigmaSeparated` literally. This makes the position-0/position-1 `Iff.rfl` collapse genuinely definitional rather than `Iff`-modulo-`Set.union_comm`, which would otherwise force a `simp` step into every downstream consumer.

**"(shortest)" deliberately dropped from item 2's existentials.** The LN's parenthetical "(shortest)" is a downstream strengthening: given any witness, the shortest one exists by well-foundedness of $\mathbb{N}$ on `Walk.length`. Baking it into the existential here would force every consumer needing just a witness to discharge the minimality side-condition, and would make this TFAE non-uniform with `sigmaOpens_TFAE`. Consumers that genuinely need the shortest witness derive it via `Nat.find` on the length-of-witness predicate.

**Strict-implicit binders in universals, explicit binders in existentials.** Item 1's `⦃v w : α⦄` mirrors the predicate's signature so `Iff.rfl` discharges positions 0 ↔ 1. Item 2's existentials use ordinary `(v w : α)` because callers destructure the witnesses, and strict-implicit on an existential would force them to recover the vertices from the walk's type.

**Collider-control asymmetry between clauses 2(a) and 2(b) preserved exactly.** Item 2 position 1 is a plain $\sigma$-open *path*; position 2 is a $\sigma$-open *walk* with the conjunct "every collider of $\pi$ is in $C$". This mirrors clauses (1) and (3) of `sigmaOpens_TFAE` literally — the path side carries no collider conjunct, the walk side carries the full one — and is what the LN's claim actually delivers between them.
